channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: T1 Peyz Records All-Time LoL Esports Pentakill Record
item.content:encoded:
T1 botlaner Kim ‘Peyz’ Su-hwan is now officially the League of Legends pro with the most pentakills in the esport’s history.

The Korean ADC recorded his 13th career League of Legends pentakill during T1’s LCK victory over Nongshim RedForce.

Peyz has now recorded at least two pentakills for every team he has played for: two for T1, two for JD Gaming, and a staggering nine at Gen.G.

At just 20 years pf age and now playing for the team that has won the last three LoL World Championships, Peyz has a chance to run away with the Pentakills record.

While Peyz is on 13 pentakills, there’s a three-way tie for second with Park ‘Ruler’ Jae-hyuk, Chen ‘GALA’ Wei, and infamous Turkish pro Anıl ‘HolyPhoenix’ Işık all having 12.

They are then closely followed by retired Vietnamese VCS star Nguyễn ‘Celebrity’ Phước Long Hiệp on 11 pentakills, and Yoon ‘Ice’ Sang-hoon as well as Western icons Yiliang ‘Doublelift’ Peng and Martin ‘Rekkles’ Larsson who each have 10.

As for champions, Kai’Sa has almost 100 more pentas than any other champion, with the top five as follows:

  • Kai’Sa (235)
  • Jinx (161)
  • Zeri (137)
  • Aphelios (105)
  • Ezreal (96)

The most from a non-ADC champion is Azir on 50, while all eight support pentakills have come on Pyke.

Thanks to the nine pentas from Peyz, Gen.G is the team with the most recorded pentakills at 18, though they would be far behind the combined 31 if Royal Club and Royal Never Give Up shared stats.

The top five of teams with the most pentakills currently looks like this:

  • Gen.G (18)
  • G2 Esports, Invictus Gaming, and Royal Club (16)
  • eSuba, Royal Never Give Up (15)
  • Team WE (14)
  • Cloud9, Fnatic (13)

What is your favourite pentakill in LoL esports history? Share your memories with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see why content creator Christopher ‘MoistCr1TiKaL’ White Jr. claims the Jynxzi LoL tournament was what the LCS needed.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1790: Hints and Answer for May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1790 for May 14, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, May 14, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1790 is an extremely tough puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 6.3 out of 6 guesses. That means the average player won’t guess the answer, and it’s over a full guess harder than the 4.8-guess average from May 13.

General Wordle Hints For May 14

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It means to hesitate or show uncertainty.
  • Hint 2: It can also describe something physically trembling or shifting slightly.
  • Hint 3: You might use it when someone changes their mind or loses resolve.
  • Hint 4: It suggests instability in decision-making or movement.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1790 for May 14, 2026.

Starter Words For May 14, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • VOWEL
  • WARES
  • RAVEN
  • WATER

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1790 On May 14, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: WAVER

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

WAVER is a verb with multiple meanings. It can mean, “to sway to and fro” or “to shake or tremble, as the hands or voice.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1789 on May 12, 2026

Answer: DOWDY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1790

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on May 15 will be Wordle Game #1791.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Next Major ARC Raiders Update Pushed to October as Embark Slows Down Content
item.content:encoded:
In a massive article on social media, the team at Embark Studios has revealed a deliberate slowing down of content updates for ARC Raiders, the revolutionary extraction adventure title released in October 2025. The team stressed that monthly content updates, which have been delivered since the game came out, are unsustainable, and the pressure is too much.

Instead, they’ll be delivering two major updates a year, starting with the next big drop, which will land in October, dubbed Frozen Trail. That confirmation also renders part of a recent set of leaks accurate.

It was stressed that a live-service model will still be front and centre, but this is all part of a conscious effort to deliver on ‘the bigger ambitions (Embark) has for this game.’

ARC Raiders Updates Are Taking a Back Burner

In a post on social media, Embark Studios explained:

Going forward, we’ve made the decision to release major updates twice a year – larger in scale, more impactful, with the goal to genuinely change how you play the game. Don’t worry, a dedicated live service team will continue running ARC Raiders day-to-day: regular live updates, plus balance fixes, bug fixes, store updates, and player events aren’t going anywhere.

The first major update, Frozen Trail, will be the biggest to hit ARC Raiders since the game came out. This follows the underwhelming introduction of Riven Tides, which many thought didn’t deliver on bold promises made.

Frozen Trail will feature an entirely new landscape comprised of the largest ARC Raiders map yet. It’ll also have a new ARC operation that features unique and challenging ARC enemies. The skill system will be redesigned and replaced with something new, and, finally, the ARC’s origins will be detailed.

This is all in addition to new weapons, items, instruments, and more.

The team explained:

It’s important to understand, we’re not just stacking new content on top of the existing experience. We’re refining core systems to support a more meaningful long-term experience – one you genuinely want to keep returning to. Frozen Trail is just the beginning of where we want to take ARC Raiders, and we’ll share more in the coming months.

More content is coming, though. From next week, a new late-game trader will be added to ARC Raiders, entering Speranza from above ground. He’ll be part of a nomadic tribe that roams the surface, and he’ll offer unique rewrads, rare items, and cosmetics, in exchange for higher value items.

Using this trader will also unlock extra stash space and an Expedition Vault that will permit you to carry up to five items over from your Expedition.

Long-term, Embark Studios wants to address progression to firm up the endgame and evolve the Expedition Project, something that has come under intense scrutiny in recent months.

It’s important to us that players understand something about where our collective ‘Embark brain’ is at: our ambitions go far beyond what players have seen so far. We want to go further in showing you the wider universe of ARC Raiders.

We think about ARC Raiders as an experience that should continue expanding over time, and right now we’re still early in that journey. The bi-annual cadence gives us the opportunity to develop to build future updates with more intention, ensuring that gameplay, progression, narrative, and the world itself all move forward together.

In the closing notes of the article, the team stressed this is just the beginning of ARC Raiders, and not a winding-down effort.

Are you surprised by this massive pivot from Embark Studios? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Crimson Desert DLC is being considered

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert DLC Being Considered by Pearl Abyss
item.content:encoded:
In a new earnings release, Pearl Abyss revealed that they are looking at ways to broaden Crimson Desert, with a DLC seemingly on the cards.

Crimson Desert has been receiving major updates since its launch, with the developers hard at work improving the gameplay experience. However, it seems their work doesn’t stop with just support, and an expansion could be on the horizon.

Crimson Desert DLC Seems Like a Possibility, Platform Expansion Mentioned As Well

During their latest earnings release, Pearl Abyss mentioned exploring a DLC for Crimson Desert in their ‘New Pipelines and Growth Strategy’ section. The developers first spoke about how their focus will remain on “enhancing user satisfaction and driving new sales through continuous updates”. The developers will also look at increasing the game’s presence through ‘Platform Expansion’ (maybe a Switch 2 port?).

The developers are also looking at ways to broaden the game, with DLC one of the routes they are exploring. However, more details will be shared “once the concrete plans are set.”

The developers also spoke about their upcoming games, DokeV and Plan 8, adding that they will maintain their goal of releasing a new title every 2-3 years. Keeping that goal in mind, resources will be prioritized for DokeV to accelerate development. DokeV is right now in the pre-production stage, while Plan 8 is in the conceptualization stage.

In other news, Pearl Abyss recently spoke about the game’s success, their approach to player feedback, and how they update the game accordingly. Additionally, the developers have explained that Crimson Desert is getting an extraction feature, but it’s not what you might expect. What are your thoughts on Crimson Desert receiving a DLC? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox Game Pass for China, which is seemingly in development. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
